title: Nutrition for Mortals
subtitle: Health, science, and common sense on your plate
author: Wordsus Team
description: Explore the secrets of health and nutrition through 52 weekly lessons. Debunk common myths, understand your body, and learn to eat with science and common sense.

chapters.title: The Five Meals Myth: Boosting Your Metabolism?
chapters.description: We analyze the myth of eating five times a day to speed up metabolism. Discover what real science says about meal frequency and your health.

chapters.title: Eggs and Cholesterol: Hero or Villain on Your Plate?
chapters.description: We debunk the old belief that eating eggs daily raises bad cholesterol, evaluating its real impact on people's health and daily nutrition.

chapters.title: Sweet Deception Tested: The Truth About Sweeteners
chapters.description: We explore the science behind stevia, aspartame, and sucralose. Are artificial sweeteners a safe alternative to classic refined sugar?

chapters.title: Juice vs. Whole Fruit: The Behavior of Sugar
chapters.description: We explain why drinking fruit juice is not the same as eating the whole fruit, and how the lack of fiber radically alters sugar's impact.

chapters.title: Nighttime Carbs: Myth or Fat-Storing Factory?
chapters.description: We review the belief that eating carbs at night makes you gain more weight. Learn how your body manages energy and the real impact of dinner time.

chapters.title: Detox Diets Examined: Commercial Magic or Physiology?
chapters.description: We analyze famous detox diets to discover if they are just a marketing strategy or if they truly help your liver and kidneys purify your body.

chapters.title: Dairy in Adults: Biological Need or Cultural Habit?
chapters.description: We address the great controversy over cow's milk consumption in adults. Are we designed to drink dairy or should we avoid it altogether?

chapters.title: Vitamins in Pills: True Allies or Wasted Money?
chapters.description: Discover if you really need to take vitamin supplements if you already eat a healthy diet, or if you are just creating rather expensive urine.

chapters.title: The Gut-Brain Axis: Your Stomach in Mental Control
chapters.description: Dive into the gut-brain axis to understand how your digestive health dictates your mood, your emotions, and your daily psychological well-being.

chapters.title: Sugar and the Brain: The Sweet Impact on Neurochemistry
chapters.description: We explore the impact of sugar consumption on brain chemistry, evaluating how it affects our emotions, learning, and natural reward systems.

chapters.title: Foods for Memory: Shielding Your Mental Health
chapters.description: Learn about key foods that improve concentration, boost long-term memory, and actively help prevent natural age-related cognitive decline.

chapters.title: Nutrition Against Anxiety: Food to Calm Your Nerves
chapters.description: We review the relationship between your diet and anxiety. Discover which specific nutrients can help regulate and calm your nervous system safely.

chapters.title: Eating for Better Sleep: Tryptophan and Deep Rest
chapters.description: We analyze the science behind tryptophan and melatonin, revealing how your food choices at dinner can guarantee you a deeply restorative rest.

chapters.title: Ultra-Processed Addiction: A Chemical Hijacking?
chapters.description: We debate whether a physiological addiction to food truly exists, focusing on how ultra-processed products manipulate our brain's self-control.

chapters.title: Coffee Under the Loupe: Benefits and Nervous Limits
chapters.description: We break down the pros and cons of coffee and caffeine consumption, analyzing its health benefits and setting limits to avoid altering sleep.

chapters.title: The Gut Microbiome: The Universe in Your Digestion
chapters.description: A complete introduction to the gut microbiome. Learn why these trillions of bacteria are called our second brain and how to care for them today.

chapters.title: Probiotics and Prebiotics: The Colon's Perfect Match
chapters.description: We explain the fundamental difference between probiotics and prebiotics, and how to combine them strategically in your meals for great digestion.

chapters.title: The Power of Fermented Foods: Kefir, Kimchi and Kombucha
chapters.description: We explore the revival of fermented foods in our modern diet and how to easily integrate them to drastically boost your overall health.

chapters.title: Leaky Gut Syndrome: Repairing Your Internal Barriers
chapters.description: Discover what leaky gut syndrome is, how it is currently diagnosed, and what specific nutritional strategies help seal the intestinal barrier.

chapters.title: The Superpower of Fiber: Much More Than Going to the Bathroom
chapters.description: We analyze why fiber is an indispensable super-nutrient that does much more than regulate transit, protecting your heart and metabolism at its root.

chapters.title: Antibiotics and Gut Flora: How to Rebuild Your Defenses
chapters.description: We show you how antibiotic treatments can wipe out your gut flora and what the exact steps are to successfully rebuild it from scratch.

chapters.title: Microbiome and Weight Loss: The Most Hidden Connection
chapters.description: Discover the fascinating relationship between the specific composition of your gut bacteria and the ease, or great difficulty, of losing weight.

chapters.title: Secrets of the Blue Zones: Nutrition of Centenarians
chapters.description: We travel to the world's Blue Zones to analyze the surprising nutritional secrets of those populations that manage to live past one hundred years.

chapters.title: Autophagy in Action: The Body's Cellular Recycling
chapters.description: We explain what autophagy is, how the body manages to recycle its own damaged cells, and how nutrition can activate this great biological benefit.

chapters.title: Antioxidants on the Attack: Fighting Cellular Wear
chapters.description: We analyze the constant cellular battle between antioxidants and free radicals, and how a correct diet significantly delays premature aging.

chapters.title: Oral Collagen Tested: Does It Work or Does Acid Kill It?
chapters.description: We solve the mystery of collagen in powder or pills. Does the stomach really destroy it before it acts, or is it an effective skin supplement?

chapters.title: Daily Nutricosmetics: Foods for Better Skin and Hair
chapters.description: Discover which foods directly and visibly impact the comprehensive health of your skin, hair, and nails, improving your aesthetics from within.

chapters.title: Caloric Restriction: The Limit to Living More Years
chapters.description: We evaluate current scientific evidence on how moderate caloric restriction has a real and proven impact on extending human life expectancy.

chapters.title: Chronic Inflammation: Fighting the Silent Enemy
chapters.description: We explore low-grade inflammation, what causes it in our day-to-day lives, and how to structure an anti-inflammatory diet to prevent diseases.

chapters.title: The Ketogenic Diet Keto: Using Fats as Fuel
chapters.description: We explain in detail the science of the keto diet, its metabolic adaptation effects, and why the body begins to use fat as its main energy.

chapters.title: Intermittent Fasting: Protocols and Common Mistakes
chapters.description: We analyze the real benefits of intermittent fasting, review its schedule protocols, and teach you how to avoid the most common beginner mistakes.

chapters.title: Plant-Based Diet: The Differences with Strict Veganism
chapters.description: We clarify what it means to follow a plant-based diet, how it differs from strict veganism, and how it greatly benefits your comprehensive health.

chapters.title: The Mediterranean Diet: The Eternal King of Nutrition
chapters.description: We analyze why the scientific community continues to crown the Mediterranean diet as the healthiest and most balanced eating style long-term.
chapters.thumbnailTitle: The best diet in the world

chapters.title: The Carnivore Diet: Analysis of a Very Extreme Trend
chapters.description: We thoroughly analyze the recent carnivore diet trend. We evaluate its supposed anecdotal benefits against its obvious clinical nutritional risks.

chapters.title: Intuitive Eating: Making Peace with Your Daily Food
chapters.description: An introduction to the intuitive eating movement, teaching you to listen to your body's signals to finally abandon the eternal diet mentality.

chapters.title: The FODMAP Diet: A Great Relief for Irritable Bowel
chapters.description: We explain how the low FODMAP diet works, the most effective clinical and structured solution to treat chronic irritable bowel syndrome.

chapters.title: The Dreaded Rebound Effect: Why Strict Diets Fail
chapters.description: Understand the physiology behind extreme restrictive diets and why they almost always trigger a fierce rebound effect that ruins your metabolism.

chapters.title: Glycemic Index: The Real Impact of Insulin Spikes
chapters.description: We explain what the glycemic index is in an easy way and how controlling insulin spikes is the true key to not constantly storing body fat.

chapters.title: Food Thermogenesis: Calories That Burn Other Calories
chapters.description: We explore diet-induced thermogenesis and discover which foods require your body to spend more energy to be properly processed and digested.

chapters.title: Anabolic Window: The Great Muscle Building Myth
chapters.description: We dismantle the myth of the urgent post-workout protein shake, clarifying how much protein you really need to maximize your muscle gain.

chapters.title: Brown Fat and White Fat: Two Sides of the Same Coin
chapters.description: We differentiate white fat from brown fat, explaining how your body is capable of utilizing one type of adipose tissue to burn off the other.

chapters.title: Quality or Quantity: The Dilemma of Counting Calories
chapters.description: We analyze the classic nutritional debate to discover if food quality truly matters more or if it's just about mathematical caloric balance.

chapters.title: Insulin Resistance: How to Reverse It by Eating Right
chapters.description: We explain exactly what insulin resistance is, how it is diagnosed, and what specific dietary changes manage to successfully reverse it.

chapters.title: The Ultra-Processed Deception: Supermarket Chemistry
chapters.description: We reveal exactly what ultra-processed products are, why they are hyper-palatable, and how the industry designs them to be highly addictive.

chapters.title: Nutritional Labels: Learn to Read What You Buy
chapters.description: We teach you a simple and practical guide to read and decipher supermarket nutritional labels without needing to be a scientist or dietitian.

chapters.title: Cooking Methods: The Thermal Impact on Your Food
chapters.description: Discover how different preparation methods, such as boiling, frying, or baking, strongly alter the amount of nutrients that reach your plate.

chapters.title: Endocrine Disruptors: The Hidden Risk of Using Plastics
chapters.description: We warn about the hidden danger of endocrine disruptors present in plastic containers and packaging that come into contact with our daily food.

chapters.title: Smart Chrononutrition: The Importance of Meal Schedules
chapters.description: We analyze the field of chrononutrition to show you why the time you consume your food impacts your body just as much as the nutrients themselves.

chapters.title: The Water Myth: The Rule of Two Liters Every Day
chapters.description: We question the rigid recommendation of drinking two liters of water daily, explaining how proper hydration truly works and should be measured.

chapters.title: Superfoods in Doubt: True Treasures or Advertising Scam
chapters.description: We put famous superfoods like chia, maca, and spirulina under the microscope. Are they a nutritional marvel or the result of brilliant marketing?

chapters.title: Natural Antinutrients: Fear of Legumes and Grains?
chapters.description: We explain what antinutrients like phytates and lectins are, and why the fear of eating grains or legumes is unjustified with good preparation.

chapters.title: Stress and Digestion: The Blockade of Your Nutrients
chapters.description: We analyze how living under chronic stress alters the digestive system, causing severe discomfort and preventing the real absorption of nutrients.
